rulings.Case Title: LYLE HUGHES VS. UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants New York Life Insurance Company, Unum Group, First Unum Life Insurance Company Motion For Summary Judgment ((Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 9, 2010, line 11, Defts New York Life Insurance Company, Unum Group, First Unum Life Insurance Company Motion For Summ. Judg. Summ. Jud. denied. Summ.Adj. grt'd in part, & denied in part. Summ. Adj. for intentional negligent mispre. grt'd. No TIF raised, no evid. that N.Y. Life or Unum representatives or employees made a false representation to plft. regarding the polices. "Touting" of the plan is not sufficient & argument re: ombudsman is insufficient because no showing Unum had no intent to perfrom on the promise or that ombudsman did not exist. Summ. Adj. as to intent. infliction of emotion distress grt'd, no triable issue of fact because no evid. of an intent to cause severe emotional distress, or that degree of distress sufficient to support C. of Act. Summ. Adj. as to Unum for breach of contract and bad faith causes of act. grt'd. No admiss. evid. presented that Unum was the reinsurer of N.Y. Life, & Delos limited to unique facts between an insurance exchange. Summ. Adj. as to bad faith against N.Y. Life grt'd. No admiss. evid. presented that claims process done in bad faith. Sargent declar. not admiss. because it makes improper legal conclusions, & settlement agreement not admiss. Plft. has not presented evid. to contradict deft's evid. that its doctors found he could still work with his condition & that he had not shown in his claim that his disability had worsened right before his claim. No evid. presented by plft. to contradict the fact he did not provide the additional medical records requested in the Jan. & Aug. letters. No TIF re: bad faith & plft. has not overcome the genuine dispute doctrine since simply denying a claim, though erroneously, does not constitute bad faith. No TIF raised regarding pun. damages. Summ. Adj. denied as to Breach of Contract against N.Y. Life. TIF raised whether pltf. under care of physicians during the year leading up to his claim for disability. (records to Giordano fr. 12/29/03 to 3/23/

rulings.Case Title: ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ELIZABETH BASTIAANSE HAMREN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 9, 2010, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM, DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action, hostile work environment/sexual harassment. Cross-complainant must plead facts demonstrating severe or pervasive workplace harassment. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to fifth cause of action, wrongful termination. Stock options are not wages or earned compensation. International Business Machines Corp. vs. Bajorek (9th Cir.) (1999) 191 F.3d 1033, 1039-1040. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MINOTT WESSINGER AS TRUSTEE OF THE MINOTT VS. EDWARD KAVOUNAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendant Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 9, 2010, line 6, Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause To Defendant Re: Preliminary Injunction. Injunction denied. Even if the court assumes an encroachment exists, defendant appears innocent. Defendant reasonabily relied upon the March 2007 survey, and the April 2009 meeting appearing to resolve any boundary dispute. Given the small nature of the encroachment, the harm to plaintiff is minor. The hardship to defendant is great. Defendant may still work on the wall if it can do so without trespassing on plaintiff's land. Defendant's request for affirmative relief denied, defendant to make formal application for such relief. Request for continuance denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN ACOSTA et al VS. PATRICK HENNING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate Memo Of P & A And Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 9, 2010, line 7, RESPONDENTS PATRICK HENNING, BONNIE GARCIA, ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, DEBBIE ENDSLEY Motion to Strike Second Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Motion to strike granted in part and denied in part. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend as to page 25, lines 17-18 "provisions of Art. 14, sec. 4 of the California Constitution" for petitioners in insert proper reference if they wish to do so. Motion granted without leave to amend as to page 25: paragraph 6; paragraph 4, line 13 "and salary reductions"; paragraph 5, line 19 "and subsequently reducing their salaries"; and paragraph 7, line 26 "and to cease and desist from any further salary reductions of EDD and CUIAB employees." Motion denied in all other respects. =(302/CWW)
